one day [is] with the Lord as a thousand years It is no statement of time, but the contrary—the simple assurance of how little God counts time as man counts it. It might be as fairly argued from it that the millennial "thousand years" was but a day, as that the creation "days" represented each a thousand years; For it is not only "One day is with the Lord as a thousand years," but also "a…

"Until our earth assumed its position among the planets, and began to perform its revolutions, and the earth was so far completed as to assume its position among the heavenly orbs, and perform its revolutions as now, present modes of reckoning time could not be appointed to man-—either our days or months or our years, all of which are determined by the revolutions of the earth upon its axis, and…

[2 Peter iii. 10.
